definition: Birth or coming-into-being, especially as conditioned by becoming in dependent origination.
notes: Birth is the default because it is clear and fits the standard sequence. Arising may be useful in explanatory prose where the broader processive sense needs emphasis, but it should not erase the force of the canonical link. The term should be heard as the outcome of becoming in the standard sequence, not as a free-floating synonym for any arising whatever. Buddhadasa Bhikkhu regularly reads jati in practical dependent-origination teaching as the repeated birth of selfing, egoic identity, or suffering in lived experience; the repository records that as a strong source-backed explanatory emphasis while keeping birth as the default rendering.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing process rather than event
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the English line benefits from it.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the movement from becoming into aging-and-death
context_rules.notes: Keep the place of the link within the chain explicit.
Notes may explicitly unpack this as the birth of I and mine or the arising of suffering in immediate experience.
